subject: [ROBOT] Disable Istio sidecar injection by default
commit_message:
[ROBOT] Disable Istio sidecar injection by default

Istio may cause problems with proper Robot deployment.

Signed-off-by: Maciej Wereski <m.wereski@partner.samsung.com>
Issue-ID: OOM-2232
Change-Id: I6fc095c7092c3b19bd61ff0691c02249ccd554e4

Patch Set 2:

Robot was deployed without any regressions (i.e. without labels that this patch adds).

Patch Set 2:

I tested robot healthcheck tests on the SM environment with and without sidecar.
both are working fine.
If we would enable the sidecar, the benefit would be, 
that we could see the communication between robot and the services in kiali.
So I would abandon this patch.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> I tested robot healthcheck tests on the SM environment with and without sidecar.
> both are working fine.
> If we would enable the sidecar, the benefit would be, 
> that we could see the communication between robot and the services in kiali.
> So I would abandon this patch.

I've tested it today (with '[ROBOT] Make Robot ServiceMesh compatible' as HEAD) and it started working for me. Still this patch doesn't turn off SM, but makes it an option (it can be enabled by default). Anyway if you think that such option isn't very handy then I can abandon it.

Abandoned

As tested the robot tests are working with Sidecar
